description_long: This is a vintage portable stereo sound system, commonly known as a boombox, featuring a rectangular body with a metallic and plastic exterior. The front face displays two large circular speaker grilles on either side, a central cassette tape deck with a transparent window, and a series of buttons and dials for audio control. A telescopic antenna extends from the top, and a sturdy handle runs along the upper edge for easy carrying. Several cassette tapes are shown nearby, emphasizing the device's primary function as a tape player. The sides and back of the unit reveal additional ports, ventilation grilles, and possibly auxiliary input/output jacks, all typical of classic urban audio equipment.
description: A vintage boombox with dual front speakers, central cassette deck, control buttons, and a top handle, accompanied by several cassette tapes. The exterior is metallic and plastic, with visible ports and a telescopic antenna.
description_view_0: Front view showing dual speaker grilles, central cassette deck, control buttons, and cassette tapes placed nearby.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the unit, the handle, and the cassette tapes positioned to the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back panel with ventilation grilles, ports, and the cassette tapes behind the unit.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing additional ports, the handle, and a clearer view of the cassette tapes on the right.
attribute_speaker_grille_pattern: Circular mesh pattern with small perforations covering the speaker areas on the front face.
attribute_control_panel_layout: A row of rectangular buttons and round knobs located above and below the cassette deck on the front, with clear separation between controls for playback, tuning, and volume.
